# `x-html`

Replace the element's body with the raw HTML result of an expression. Nothing is escaped — the template author is responsible for the safety of the input.

## Syntax

```
x-html="expression"
```

## Value rules

| Value type | Result |
|---|---|
| `string` | Emitted verbatim as the body content |
| `null`, `undefined` | Nothing emitted (empty body) |
| Anything else | `ReflowRuntimeError` |

## Semantics

- Existing children of the element are ignored at render time.
- Only one of `x-text`, `x-html`, `x-include` may appear on the same element.
- Combines with `x-data`, `x-bind`, structural, and iteration directives.
- No parsing, no validation — the string becomes part of the output byte-for-byte.

## When to use it

`x-html` is intentionally the last resort. Prefer `x-text` whenever the value is user-controlled or you can express the markup with template structure. Reach for `x-html` when:

- A trusted upstream produced sanitized HTML (server-rendered Markdown, editorial CMS output that has been sanitized).
- You need to emit content that Reflow's directives cannot describe (SVG fragments generated by a helper, embedded JSON island, etc.).

## Common uses

### Sanitized rich text

```html
<article x-html="sanitize($.post.body_html)"></article>
```

### JSON island for a client script

```html
<script type="application/json" id="page-data" x-html="jsonStringify($.pageData)"></script>
```

### Prerendered SVG

```html
<div class="chart" x-html="renderSvgChart($.series)"></div>
```

## Failure modes

| Trigger | Error |
|---|---|
| Expression syntax error at compile | `ReflowCompileError` |
| Value is not a string / null / undefined | `ReflowRuntimeError` |
| Helper throws | `ReflowRuntimeError { cause: <original> }` |

## Security note

Because the string is inserted unescaped, `x-html` is a common source of XSS if used carelessly. Guarantees to check before using it:

- The value is generated by a trusted source (never user input directly).
- If it does contain user input, the helper that produced it has sanitized appropriately for the target context.
- The surrounding markup does not open new attack surfaces (event handlers, `<script>`, `<style>` scoped inserts).

Reflow does not ship a sanitizer — call out to one from a helper if you need it.
